asks: 3D Geometry – Exam Ready Notes Distance Between Two Points 3D Geometry – Exam Ready Notes 1. Distance Between Two Points Given points P(x1, y1, z1) and Q(x2, y2, z2): d = √[(x2 − x1)² + (y2 − y1)² + (z2 − z1)²] 2. Direction Cosines If a line makes angles α, β, γ with x, y, z axes: l = cosα, m = cosβ, n = cosγ l² + m² + n² = 1 3. Equation of a Line Vector Form: r = a + λb Cartesian Form: (x − x1)/l = (y − y1)/m = (z − z1)/n 4. Equation of a Plane General Form: Ax + By + Cz + D = 0 Normal Form: x cosα + y cosβ + z cosγ = p Practice Problems (JEE Style) 1 Find the distance between points (1, 2, 3) and (4, −2, 5). 2 Find direction cosines of a line equally inclined to all axes. 3 Find equation of line passing through (1, 2, 3) with direction ratios (2, −1, 2). 4 Find angle between planes: 2x + y − z = 3 and x − y + 2z = 5. 5 Find shortest distance from point (2, −1, 4) to plane x + 2y + 2z = 9. Key Tip: Always normalize direction ratios when needed and remember dot product rules for angles

equations.raw: l = cosα, m = cosβ, n = cosγ
